releases.title: v3.0.0
releases.summary: This major release completes the frontend build modernization from Create React App to Vite. The app now builds and runs through a single Vite-based toolchain for local development and production static assets, the nginx-served frontend image uses Vite output directly, the legacy CRA fallback rail is removed, and the repo has been re-proven against the live Docker stack and browser-regression suite on the final Vite-only state.

releases.title: v2.11.0
releases.summary: This minor release adds the first optional market valuation foundation for collectZ. Workspace-scoped integrations can now carry valuation provider configuration, media records can store normalized read-only value bands, the library detail drawer can display and manually refresh valuation data, and imports can queue non-blocking follow-on valuation jobs for newly created media. The milestone keeps pricing optional, read-only, and safely decoupled from core catalog CRUD and import flows.

releases.title: v2.10.17
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the workspace-owned member lifecycle foundation. Workspace owners and admins can now force password resets, suspend and restore members within the workspace boundary, invite a brand-new person to become the initial owner during workspace creation, and remove a member from a workspace without silently deleting the content they contributed there. The release keeps platform-global administration distinct from workspace-scoped lifecycle control and preserves the intended tenancy model.

releases.title: v2.10.16
releases.summary: This patch release turns the SaaS auth surface into a real public account-entry flow. Users can now request password resets from the sign-in screen, self-register when the platform allows it, verify their email before normal sign-in, and land in the correct workspace model depending on how they were onboarded. Public self-registration now creates a personal workspace after verification, while invite-based onboarding still lands a new user only in the invited workspace without creating an extra personal one.

releases.title: v2.10.15
releases.summary: This patch release establishes a real platform-owned SMTP delivery model for the SaaS product. Platform operators can now see whether email delivery is configured, manage a masked app-level SMTP override on top of environment defaults, and validate live delivery with a protected test-send flow before the next registration, password-reset, and workspace invite milestones build on top of it.

releases.title: v2.10.14
releases.summary: This patch release turns the product’s space-separation promise into real workspace behavior. Settings, integrations, and activity now belong to the current workspace instead of quietly depending on global admin surfaces, while the platform layer is narrowed toward true platform concerns.

releases.title: v2.10.13
releases.summary: This patch release finishes the remaining `2.10.x` shared-language cleanup by moving the app away from display-font chrome and auth-page mini-marketing toward calmer, more product-native shared UI. Workspace headings, panel headings, and the authentication surface now follow the same quieter language instead of drifting into uppercase dashboard theatrics.

releases.title: v2.10.12
releases.summary: This patch release removes the remaining eyebrow-style uppercase language from the shared form label primitive so forms throughout the app inherit calmer, more product-native labeling by default. Instead of continuing to reintroduce tracked uppercase microheadings through the shared form layer, drawers, admin screens, auth flows, and supporting forms now share a quieter label treatment from the root.

releases.title: v2.10.11
releases.summary: This patch release tones down the app’s remaining heavy overlay shells so the camera modal and the main slide-in drawers feel calmer and more functional. The overlays still focus attention and behave the same way, but they no longer rely as heavily on blur, oversized rounding, or dramatic shell/poster shadowing to create that sense of separation.

releases.title: v2.10.10
releases.summary: This patch release makes the add/edit drawers feel more like normal working surfaces and less like staged modal moments. The editor header keeps the same structure and actions, but the title no longer shouts in forced all-caps display styling and instead reads like a calmer product workspace heading.
